title: Meki Memory Agent in Elixir
summary: A faithful tour of MeKi (Memory-based Expert Knowledge Injection, arXiv 2602.03359) — scaling an LLM's capacity with cheap ROM storage instead of FLOPs — then a runnable re-implementation in Elixir's Nx & Axon: token-level memory experts, the low-rank additive-sigmoid gate, the re-parameterization trick that folds training into a zero-cost lookup table, and serving it with Nx.Serving. Architecture and code are real; benchmark numbers are quoted from the paper, not reproduced.

images.prompt: A serene, modern editorial illustration in flat vector style: a smartphone-sized silicon die seen from above, where a dim amber grid of tiny memory cells (a ROM lookup table) glows and sends a single bright thread of light up into a translucent indigo 'transformer' block, running alongside a busy compute unit — suggesting capacity drawn from storage rather than arithmetic. Cool slate-and-indigo palette with warm amber ROM highlights and a single violet accent, generous whitespace, soft depth, no text baked in.
images.alt: A glowing lookup table of memory cells feeding a transformer layer in parallel with its arithmetic unit
images.caption: Capacity drawn from a glowing ROM lookup table, in parallel with the arithmetic unit.

images.prompt: A clean two-panel editorial tech illustration, flat vector with soft glow. Left panel: a busy training-time block — a small neural projector (interlocking gears and a branching SwiGLU shape) feeding a memory grid, lots of indigo arithmetic. Right panel: the gears have dissolved into a single calm amber lookup table of glowing memory cells in ROM, with one bright row being read out — same output, no machinery. A curved arrow folds the left panel into the right. Cool slate-and-indigo palette with warm amber ROM highlights, generous whitespace, no real text baked in.
images.alt: A training-time block with a non-linear projector collapsing into a single static ROM table for inference
images.caption: The projector folds away; only a static lookup table survives into inference.
